description: SECOND T12 PvP report (2026-05-24 00:27:45, #148 X:597 Y:597, WOS_BattleReports_T12_146_v2.pdf). Marty 191 (attacker/rally, [RFJ]) VICTORY over GFG Achamela garrison - the SAME opponent as report_001, fought MINUTES apart (report_001 same day). NEAR-EVEN, razor-thin: attacker won with 118,068 survivors of 1,805,272 (6.54%), defender FULLY WIPED. CRITICAL PAIR WITH report_001: identical panels, identical attacker total (1,805,272), near-identical defender, SAME special bonuses - the ONLY material difference is troop COMPOSITION - yet the outcome differs wildly (this: 25 turns, LANCERS survive; report_001: 16 turns, MARKSMAN survive). The two together are the corpus's controlled near-even experiment and expose the engine's casualty-allocation gap.

attacker.survivor_composition: ALL LANCERS (118,068). Infantry and Marksman fully wiped.

stats_note: panel_pct IDENTICAL to report_001 (same players/setup minutes apart). FULL SCOUTED. Special bonuses identical to report_001 (attacker +20% troop A/D/L/H, -20% enemy A/D, Rally Atk+15%/Leth+15% widgets, pets +10% all & enemy pet penalties; defender same but Defender Def+15%/Leth+15% widgets). t12_skill_levels ASSUMED same as report_001 (not separately visible in this report - same player, minutes apart). Attacker OFFENSE-focused, defender DEFENSE-focused.
reconstructed_timeline_2026_07_05: Martin reconstructed from hero-skill trigger counts. ROBUST anchors: (1) battle = 25 turns [Vulcanus SK3 'every 3 turns' triggered 9x -> turns 1,4,7,...,25 -> (9-1)*3+1=25]. (2) his infantry died end T19 [his Elif SK3 = 19]. (3) enemy infantry died end T22 [enemy Elif SK3 = 22]. (4) enemy marksman died ~T18 [enemy Cara 'every 2' = 9x -> 9*2=18]. (5) his LANCERS are the sole survivors (118,068). WRINKLE: Vulcanus SK2 (revised to 'every 5 attacks PER TROOP TYPE') = 10 triggers implies his MARKSMAN died ~T10-14 [inf floor(19/5)=3 + lancer floor(25/5)=5 + marks=2 -> 3+5+2=10], i.e. his marksman died EARLY. RESOLVED (Martin): his MARKSMAN were annihilated by ~T14 by enemy lancer Ambusher procs + enemy Cara SK3 bursts (bypass the front, hit marksman directly), NON-LINEARLY (step drops at each proc), while his LANCERS stayed IMMORTAL behind infantry until infantry died T19, then survived to T25 (118,068). So death order his side: MARKSMAN (~T14, via bypass procs) -> INFANTRY (T19, front wall) -> LANCER survives. permanent LOSSES=33,199 (non-zero here vs 0 in report_001).
engine_finding_2026_07_05: CORRECTED (Martin: absorption IS strict front-to-back - the ORDER mechanic is RIGHT, not a bug). The real gap: the engine models hero skills as AVERAGED modifiers (Vulcanus SK2 +100%dd/freq5 -> ~+20% avg; Cara SK3 +40%dd/freq2 -> ~+20% avg) and does NOT fire discrete procs, so it cannot reproduce BACKLINE ANNIHILATION VIA BYPASS PROCS. report_002: his MARKSMAN (626k) were annihilated by ~T14 by enemy Ambusher procs + enemy Cara SK3 bursts (both bypass the front, hit marksman directly), while his LANCERS stayed immortal behind infantry until T19 then survived (118,068). The engine's only bypass is a fixed 20% ambush fraction (LANCER_BYPASS_CHANCE) - too weak - and Cara SK3 is not modeled as a backline hit, so the engine keeps marksman alive in BOTH battles (always returns MARKSMAN survivor). TWO real gaps: (1) skills must FIRE discretely turn-by-turn (with correct bypass magnitudes) to reproduce NON-LINEAR proc-driven attrition + the correct survivor type; averaging cannot. (2) near-balance hypersensitivity/bimodality (exact survivor% is chaotic near even). PATH = re-architect to a turn-by-turn skill-firing simulator (which also emits the trigger+kill telemetry the front-end now wants). Both battles are the validation anchors.
